input: Our API returns 500 errors intermittently -- about 5% of requests to /api/orders fail with 'ConnectionResetError: [Errno 104] Connection reset by peer'. It only happens during peak traffic (2-4 PM). No code changes were deployed recently. The error comes from our database query layer.

After deploying commit abc123, user login started failing with 'InvalidSignatureError' on JWT verification. The commit only changed the user profile endpoint, not auth. Staging works fine. Production fails for all users. The JWT secret is stored in an environment variable.

A Python function that calculates shipping costs returns wrong results for orders with exactly 10 items. Orders with 9 items work, orders with 11 items work, but 10-item orders are always $5 too high. The function uses a tiered pricing table: 1-9 items = rate A, 10-24 items = rate B, 25+ items = rate C.

Our e-commerce site has a bug where occasionally two users can purchase the last item in stock simultaneously, resulting in overselling. The inventory check passes for both users, then both orders are created. This happens about once a week during high traffic.

After upgrading Python from 3.10 to 3.12, our data processing pipeline produces different numerical results. The output CSV has floating point differences starting around the 15th decimal place, but these accumulate through our aggregation pipeline and result in final numbers being off by up to $0.03. No code was changed, only the Python version.
